channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: I am not sure what features I prefer on the WP500 Ultra: 1TB storage, a privacy kill switch, or the ubercool Predator-style thermal imaging vision
item.description: The WP500 Ultra merges thermal imaging, hardware privacy, extreme durability, massive storage, and flagship specifications into a rugged smartphone costing $529.

item.title: Please Just Let the Metaverse Go
item.description: Google and Epic Games aren't ready to let the metaverse die.

item.title: Lawmakers just advanced online safety laws that require age verification at the app store
item.description: A package of child safety bills is headed to the House floor following an hours-long session that left Democrats and Republicans divided. On Thursday, lawmakers on the House Committee on Energy and Commerce voted to advance the Kids Internet and Digital Safety (KIDS) Act; Sammy's Law; and the App Store Accountability Act, which would require […]

item.title: DiligenceSquared uses AI, voice agents to make M&A research affordable
item.description: Instead of relying on expensive management consultants, the startup uses AI voice agents to conduct interviews with customers of the companies the PE firms are considering buying.

item.title: ‘Girl Dinner’ Takes on a Deliciously Grisly New Meaning in This Speculative Short Story
item.description: Read Jilly Dreadful's 'Eat, Prey, Love: A Modest Proposal for Ensuring Gender Equality Through Selective Dietary Practices; or, a Geriatric Millennial’s Guide to #GirlDinner' right here on io9.

item.title: The Pentagon formally labels Anthropic a supply-chain risk
item.description: After weeks of failed negotiations, public ultimatums, and lawsuit threats, the Defense Department has formally labeled Anthropic a "supply-chain risk", escalating its fight with the AI company over their acceptable use policies and potentially bringing their fight to court. The decision, first reported by The Wall Street Journal on Thursday, citing one source familiar, will […]...

item.title: Amazon.com is on the mend after experiencing technical issues
item.description: Amazon's website appears to be stabilizing after experiencing technical issues that kept users from logging in and prevented prices from displaying correctly. DownDetector reported a spike of outage reports around 2PM ET, but as of 5:56PM ET, user complaints have fallen significantly.The Amazon.com homepage currently loads, and Engadgets staff have been able to load product pages and view prices without any problems. During the peak of the site’s issues, neither were loading consistently, and clicking through in some cases showed an error page with text that says "Sorry, something went wrong o...

item.title: The next Xbox is codenamed Project Helix, and we hope the Xbox CEO's 'commitment to the return of Xbox' is real
item.description: Xbox CEO Asha Sharma has revealed that the next-generation Xbox is codenamed Project Helix and says it will “lead in performance,” with rumors pointing to a powerful new console arriving in 2027.

item.title: Cluely CEO Roy Lee admits to publicly lying about revenue numbers last year
item.description: The $7 million in annual recurring revenue that Cluely CEO Roy Lee shared last summer was a lie, its founder and CEO Roy Lee admitted on Thursday on X.

item.title: How to watch Australian Grand Prix 2026 F1: free stream, preview, schedule
item.description: All the ways to watch Australian Grand Prix 2026 live streams online and from anywhere, as F1's new season introduces the biggest regulation shake-up in years.

item.title: Here’s Every Country Directly Impacted by the War on Iran
item.description: As the conflict in the Middle East continues to escalate, more than a dozen countries in the region have reportedly been affected by air strikes.

item.title: This solar-powered laptop that you can dunk in a pool has something surprising that no rivals can offer
item.description: The Oukitel RG14-P combines rugged durability, solar charging, hot swappable batteries, and integrated lighting for demanding industrial field environments.

item.title: ‘Uncanny Valley’: Iran War in the AI Era, Prediction Market Ethics, and Paramount Beats Netflix
item.description: In this episode, our hosts unpack the ongoing conflict in the Middle East, particularly as the AI industry has been entrenching itself with the Department of Defense.

item.title: ‘One Piece’ Straw Hat Pirates, Ranked
item.description: Not all nakama are created equal, so here's our ranking of our favorite Straw Hats from least to most favorite.

item.title: OpenAI Had Banned Military Use. The Pentagon Tested Its Models Through Microsoft Anyway
item.description: Sources allege the Defense Department experimented with Microsoft’s version of OpenAI technology before the ChatGPT-maker lifted its prohibition on military applications.

item.title: 'Affordable' it is not — Nagra's music streamer and DAC costs thousands, but you know you want it anyway
item.description: Nagra's Compact Player might have you thinking about CD transports, but it's much more into the hi-res streaming game

item.title: Kevin O’Leary says Gen Z sends this ‘horrific signal’ to employers
item.description: The ‘Shark Tank’ star said in a recent interview that doing this at a job interview is a huge no-no. Don’t bring your mom or dad to an interview with Shark Tank investor Kevin O’Leary if you were planning on it.

item.title: Sam Altman Says ‘Government Should Be More Powerful Than Corporations.’ Which Government?
item.description: Being an international software giant with singular loyalty to Donald Trump is an impossible mission.

item.title: AWS launches a new AI agent platform specifically for healthcare
item.description: AWS is launching Amazon Connect Health, an AI agent platform that will help with patient scheduling, documentation, and patient verification.
